name: [BUG-GW-004] TC-GW-005 — Required security response headers must be present on all API routes
description:
**[KNOWN BUG — BUG-GW-004]**

**What this test does:**
Checks that the following security headers are present on the spr-fetcher status response:

| Header | Required Value | Purpose |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| X-Content-Type-Options | nosniff | Prevents MIME-type sniffing attacks |
| X-Frame-Options | DENY or SAMEORIGIN | Prevents clickjacking |
| Strict-Transport-Security | max-age >= 31536000 | Enforces HTTPS-only connections |
| X-XSS-Protection | 0 (disabled — modern browsers use CSP) | Legacy XSS protection |

**Actual:** None of these headers are present on any API response.

**Impact:**
- Without X-Content-Type-Options: browsers may interpret a JSON response as executable script in some edge cases
- Without Strict-Transport-Security: users on the same network can be downgraded to HTTP via a man-in-the-middle attack, exposing Bearer tokens in transit
- Without X-Frame-Options: API responses could be embedded in hidden iframes to perform clickjacking on authenticated users

**This is an OWASP A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration finding.**

**Fix:** Add these headers via the APISIX response-rewrite plugin on all routes.
descriptionHtml:
[KNOWN BUG — BUG-GW-004]

What this test does:
Checks that the following security headers are present on the spr-fetcher status response:

HeaderRequired ValuePurpose
X-Content-Type-OptionsnosniffPrevents MIME-type sniffing attacks
X-Frame-OptionsDENY or SAMEORIGINPrevents clickjacking
Strict-Transport-Securitymax-age >= 31536000Enforces HTTPS-only connections
X-XSS-Protection0 (disabled — modern browsers use CSP)Legacy XSS protection

Actual: None of these headers are present on any API response.

Impact:

  • Without X-Content-Type-Options: browsers may interpret a JSON response as executable script in some edge cases
  • Without Strict-Transport-Security: users on the same network can be downgraded to HTTP via a man-in-the-middle attack, exposing Bearer tokens in transit
  • Without X-Frame-Options: API responses could be embedded in hidden iframes to perform clickjacking on authenticated users

This is an OWASP A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration finding.

Fix: Add these headers via the APISIX response-rewrite plugin on all routes.
